cycles.description: Major differences: 1) The HiTeXeR ellipse appears much wider/flatter (wrong aspect ratio) - it extends to the edges horizontally but is too short vertically. The reference shows a tall ellipse that extends roughly from y=-4 to y=4. 2) The grid and axes are cropped incorrectly in HiTeXeR - it only shows roughly the top-right portion of what should be visible. The reference shows the full grid from approximately -6 to 6 on both axes. 3) The ellipse in HiTeXeR looks like it has wrong scaling - xscale(5)*yscale(4) should produce an ellipse 10 units wide and 8 units tall, but HiTeXeR's ellipse appears inverted (wider than tall instead of taller than wide). 4) Labels are cut off or positioned incorrectly in HiTeXeR.
cycles.fix_prompt: The Circle transformation with yscale(4)*xscale(5) is being applied incorrectly. In asy-interp.js, the order of scale transformations or their application to the Circle path is wrong. The yscale should make the circle 4x taller and xscale should make it 5x wider, resulting in an ellipse that is 10 units wide and 8 units tall. Check the transform composition in applyTransform or the Circle path generation - the scales appear to be swapped or the transform matrix multiplication order is incorrect. Also check the limits/Crop functionality which appears to be incorrectly cropping the visible area.

changes.old_code:
// Transform * transform
    if (isTransform(left) && isTransform(right)) return composeTransforms(right, left);
changes.new_code:
// Transform * transform
    if (isTransform(left) && isTransform(right)) return composeTransforms(left, right);

cycles.description: Major differences: 1) The ellipse shape is completely wrong - HiTeXeR shows a wide/flat ellipse (wider than tall) while the reference shows a tall ellipse (taller than wide). The yscale(4)*xscale(5) transformation should produce an ellipse 10 units wide and 8 units tall, but HiTeXeR renders it as wider than tall. 2) The grid/canvas is cropped incorrectly in HiTeXeR - the bottom portion of the graph is cut off, only showing roughly the top half (from about y=0 to y=6) instead of the full range y=-6 to y=6. 3) The axis labels (-5,0), (5,0), F1, F2 appear partially cut off or positioned incorrectly in HiTeXeR due to the cropping issue. 4) The y-axis arrow and lower gridlines are not visible in HiTeXeR.
cycles.fix_prompt: Two issues need fixing in asy-interp.js: 1) The yscale/xscale transformation order or application to Circle is inverted - when yscale(4)*xscale(5)*Circle((0,0),1) is evaluated, yscale should stretch vertically by 4 and xscale horizontally by 5, but the result shows the opposite effect (5 vertical, 4 horizontal or similar). Check how the transform composition yscale(4)*xscale(5) is being built and applied to the path - the transforms may be swapped. 2) The limits/Crop functionality is not working correctly - the view should show from (-6.5,-6.5) to (6.5,6.5) but HiTeXeR is only showing approximately the upper portion. Check the limits() function and Crop handling to ensure the full specified bounds are rendered, not just a partial view.
